name: Cole Porter
abstract: Cole Albert Porter (June 9, 1891 – October 15, 1964) was an American composer and songwriter. Many of his songs became standards noted for their witty, urbane lyrics, and many of his scores found success on Broadway and in Hollywood films.

about_quotes.speaker: Daryl Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: There was a plaque that was engraved that Cole Porter lived in The Waldorf Towers... I always tell this — people would come in sometimes and they look at that... I have to tell them the bad news. I didn’t always want to tell them he died so I’d tell them, “He’s playing a bar mitzvah.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Daryl Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: Granted Cole Porter may not have written the word “that,” but I think Frank knew some stuff that a lot of people didn't know. And actually there's a very funny story because Cole Porter got upset with Frank changing some of the words...
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I’m trying to do that, sometimes successfully, sometimes not. Dave Frishberg does it real well; Bob Dorough does it real well, you know. There’s a few. I’ reminded of when Cole Porter and Irving Berlin had dinner together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: I think we’re living in a less sophisticated age in terms of music... there are no songwriters today writing with the sophistication of Cole Porter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Easley
about_quotes.evidence: Well naturally it’s all the American Songbook. Gershwin, Cole Porter, you know, Sophisticated — Duke Ellington. We played the same songs...
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Easley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 1, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Cole Porter, George Gershwin I haven't even mentioned. There’s just – you know, what a tradition we have in back of us of songwriting
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
